summary: Solid soundboard capture with occasional drop‑outs, highlighted by a stellar Foolish Heart opener and a standout Dark Star in the second set.
review: The first set delivers a classic blues‑heavy opening with Foolish Heart and a fan‑favorite Little Red Rooster, while the second set rewards listeners with one of the era’s best Dark Stars.  The mix is generally clear and better than many contemporary releases, though listeners note a few drop‑outs and skips in Red Rooster, Big River and the start of Foolish Heart.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical performance (strong openings, Dark Star highlight) while acknowledging recurring drop‑outs; the overall balance of a good show with decent but imperfect recording yields a rating in the low‑mid‑4s.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Drop‑outs and skips noted in Little Red Rooster, Big River and at the start of Foolish Heart; otherwise the mix is clear, balanced and better than many other set recordings.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set opens strong with Foolish Heart and stays blues‑y through Little Red Rooster and Big River; the second set builds to a high point with a memorable Dark Star, making the overall pacing satisfying despite some reviewers finding the first set “nothing special.”

band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboards and vocals are praised as ‘smoking’ on this show.
